methods.description: Process for collecting sediment samples from the Vistula Lagoon to analyze faunal composition and environmental conditions.

steps.description: The samples were collected at two sites, in the eastern (54.39190°N, 19.68360°E) and in the middle (54.35010°N, 19.48480°E) part of the open-water area of the Polish part of the lagoon at a depth of approximately 3 m.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from specified coordinates in Vistula Lagoon

methods.description: Identification of invertebrate species and calculation of their density in sediment samples to understand faunal composition.

steps.description: Tubificinae and the majority of chironomid larvae were identified according to Timm (1999) and Andersen et al. (2013), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: identify invertebrate species using reference guides

steps.description: The species affiliation of Chironomus was adopted based on karyological studies by Markiyanova (2018).
steps.searchable_summary: determine Chironomus species using karyological studies

steps.description: The density of invertebrates in subsequent layers was expressed per sediment L to allow comparison of invertebrates abundance.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate invertebrate density per sediment liter

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including tests for co-occurrence, associations, and effects of sediment depth.

steps.description: Pearson's Chi-Square Test for 2 × 2 tables was performed to check the significance of co-occurrence of the groups.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Pearson's Chi-Square Test for co-occurrence significance

steps.description: Associations between densities of the fauna and maximum burrowing depth was identified using linear regression.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear regression for fauna density and burrowing depth

steps.description: Shapiro-Wilk test for normal distribution and Levene test for equal population variances were conducted.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Shapiro-Wilk and Levene tests for distribution and variance

steps.description: Kruskal-Wallis test was applied for the determination of the effect of sediment depth on the content of organic matter and densities of bacteria.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Kruskal-Wallis test for sediment depth effects

steps.description: Friedman test was applied to test the effect of sediment depth on the density of C. balatonicus and Tubificinae.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Friedman test for sediment depth effects on species density

steps.description: Wilcoxon signed-rank test was applied to check the significance of differences in the densities of C. balatonicus and Tubificinae between two sampling years.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Wilcoxon signed-rank test for density differences between years
inputs: species density data from two years
